que: What do integration tests mainly verify?
options: That multiple app parts work together correctly
That a single variable can be reassigned
That only UI colors are correct

que: In Android testing guidance, medium tests usually focus on what?
options: Integration between two or more units

que: Which test type usually verifies a whole user flow?

options: On the development machine or server
On a real or emulated Android device

que: Which is a good integration test example?
options: Testing a repository with a real Room database
Checking only whether a String is empty
Verifying the spelling of a variable name

que: What is a good testing strategy for integration tests?
options: Use the smallest scope that still verifies the collaboration
Always test the full app for every case
Use real network calls in every test

que: Why should real network calls usually be avoided in integration tests?
options: They can make tests slow and flaky
They are required only for local tests

que: What is a common mistake in integration testing?
options: Testing too many unrelated concerns in one test
Choosing a medium test for component collaboration

que: How does Android classify tests most importantly for app developers?
options: By what they test and where they run
By whether they use Kotlin or Java
